short_def: A network of independently owned websites that link to and reinforce each other, each documenting its own build in public while contributing to a shared, machine-readable map of the federation.
definition:
The AI Digital Karma Federation is a group of separately owned web properties, including AI Now Guide, that are deliberately linked together as a recognizable network. Each site keeps its own identity, content, and ownership, but contributes to a shared registry so that both humans and AI systems can see how the properties relate to one another.

For AI Now Guide specifically, this means sessions and articles can reference sibling properties where relevant, and the site carries a sidebar presence pointing back to the federation registry, so visitors (and crawlers) can discover the wider network.
example: A session about rebuilding a site's architecture might reference how a sibling property in the federation handled the same problem, with a link back to that site. The federation registry itself, hosted externally, lists the member properties and how they connect.
nuance: Federation does not mean shared ownership or a single brand. Each property remains independently owned and operated. The federation is a transparency layer: a way of making real relationships between separately owned sites explicit and verifiable, rather than hidden or implied.

seo.title: AI Digital Karma Federation | AI Now Guide Glossary
seo.description: What the AI Digital Karma Federation is: a network of independently owned sites, including AI Now Guide, linked through a shared registry.
